library(phyloseq)
library(decontam)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# Extract fecal samples
metaphlan <- read.csv(file="../240827mpa_all_samples_species_only.csv", header=T, row.names=1)
full_data <- read.csv(file = "../full_data.csv", row.names = 1)

## Create taxa and meta files for fecal samples

# STEP 1: Extract fecal Samples from Full Metadata 
meta_fecal <- full_data[grepl("faeces", full_data$sample_info, ignore.case = TRUE), ]

#STEP 2: Transpose Metaphlan Data
metaphlan1 <- t(metaphlan)
rownames(metaphlan1) <- make.unique(rownames(metaphlan1))

#STEP 3: Clean Row Names in Metaphlan Data
rownames(metaphlan1) <- gsub("114_RoMaNs__|__E.*", "", rownames(metaphlan1))
rownames(metaphlan1) <- gsub("^X", "", rownames(metaphlan1))

#STEP 4: Match Sample IDs Between Datasets

# Extract the row names from metaphlan1
metaphlan1_row_names <- rownames(metaphlan1)

# Extract the sampleID values from meta
meta_fecal_ID <- meta_fecal$sampleID

# Add sampleIDs to row names in meta
rownames(meta_fecal) <- meta_fecal$sampleID

# Identify which row names in metaphlan1 match any sampleID in meta
matching_row_names <- metaphlan1_row_names[metaphlan1_row_names %in% meta_fecal_ID]
     

# STEP 5: Subset Metaphlan Data for Skin Samples

# Subset metaphlan1 to include only rows with matching row names
taxa_fecal_spike <- metaphlan1[rownames(metaphlan1) %in% matching_row_names, ]

# STEP 6: Save the Extracted Data

# Save taxa with spike
write.csv(taxa_fecal_spike, "taxa_fecal_spike.csv")

# Save meta 
write.csv(meta_fecal, "meta_fecal.csv")

# STEP 1: Create dataframe with skin spike-in 

# Identify the column that contains "acidiphilus"
spike_in <- grep("acidiphilus", colnames(taxa_fecal_spike), value = TRUE)

# Create a new data frame with the identified column
fecal_spike <- taxa_fecal_spike[, spike_in, drop = FALSE]

# Rename the column to "spike_in"
colnames(fecal_spike) <- "spike_in"

#Save skin spike only
write.csv(fecal_spike, "taxa_fecal_spike_only.csv")

# STEP 2: Create dataframe without spike-in

# Remove spike-in from skin taxa
taxa_fecal <- taxa_fecal_spike[, !grepl("acidiphilus", colnames(taxa_fecal_spike))]

# Save taxa without spike
write.csv(taxa_fecal, "taxa_fecal.csv")

# Compare Samples in meta and taxa 

# STEP 1: Columns and rows in taxa

# Get the number of columns in taxa_fecal_spike
ncol(taxa_fecal_spike) #3026

# Get the number of columns in taxa_fecal
ncol(taxa_fecal) #3025

# Get the number of rows in taxa_fecal
num_rows <- nrow(taxa_fecal)
print(num_rows) #558

# STEP 2: Sample ID comparison between taxa and meta

# Display the sample IDs that are in meta but missing from taxa
set_diff = setdiff(meta_fecal$sampleID, rownames(taxa_fecal))
set_diff 

# Create meta file with spike-in reads removal

# STEP 1: Merge datasets

# Merge meta_fecal with spike-in abundances based on Sample ID
meta_fecal_spike <- merge(meta_fecal, fecal_spike, by="row.names")

# STEP 2: Calculate total reads without spike in

# Multiply total reads by spike-in relative abundance to get the number of spike-in reads
meta_fecal_spike
after_kraken2_host_removal * 0.01 * meta_fecal_spike$spike_in

# Calculate remaining reads without spike-in
meta_fecal_spike
after_kraken2_host_removal - meta_fecal_spike$spike_in_reads

# STEP 3: Set sampleID back to rownames

# Set first column as row names
row.names(meta_fecal_spike) <- meta_fecal_spike[, 1]

# Remove the first column since it's now set as row names
meta_fecal_spike <- meta_fecal_spike[, -1]

#Save meta with spike
write.csv(meta_fecal_spike, "meta_fecal_spike_reads.csv")
     
# Read in datasets

# 1. Full taxa (with spike) with negative controls  
taxa_neg_t <- read.csv(file="../240827mpa_all_samples_species_only.csv", header=T, row.names = 1)
taxa_neg <- t(taxa_neg_t)

# 2. Meta with spike-in reads + negative controls
full_meta_spike <- read.csv(file="../full_meta_spike.csv", header=T, row.names=1)

# Extract negative and fecal samples from meta file

# Extract rows where sampleID contains "neg" and sample_info contains "faeces"
meta_fecal_neg <- full_meta_spike %>%
  filter(grepl("neg", sampleID) | grepl("faeces", sample_info))

# Create the sample_or_control column based on sampleID
meta_fecal_neg <- meta_fecal_neg %>%
  mutate(sample_or_control = ifelse(grepl("neg", sampleID), "control", "sample"))

# Set a constant DNA concentration (50 ng for all samples)
meta_fecal_neg$fixed_dna_conc <- 50

# Create taxa neg file

# Make taxa_neg a dataframe
taxa_neg <- as.data.frame(taxa_neg)

# Create a sampleID column from the row names of taxa_neg
taxa_neg$sampleID <- rownames(taxa_neg)

# Reorder columns to make sampleID the first column
taxa_neg <- taxa_neg[, c("sampleID", setdiff(names(taxa_neg), "sampleID"))]

# Remove patterns from sampleID
taxa_neg$sampleID <- gsub(pattern = "X114_RoMaNs__", replacement = "", taxa_neg$sampleID)

# Remove the part after __ for rows not containing "neg" or "zymomock" in sampleID
taxa_neg$sampleID <- ifelse(
  grepl("neg|zymomock", taxa_neg
sampleID, 
  sub("__.*", "", taxa_neg$sampleID)
)

# Convert rownames to sampleID
row.names(taxa_neg) <- taxa_neg$sampleID
     

# Extract neg and fecal samples from taxa

# Filter taxa_neg to keep only rows where sampleID matches meta_fecal_neg
taxa_fecal_neg <- taxa_neg %>%
  filter(sampleID %in% meta_fecal_neg$sampleID)


Decontam with spike
Step 1: Spike

# Check if spike-in (Alicyclobacillus) is in the column names
is_in_column_name <- any(grepl("Alicyclobacillus", colnames(taxa_fecal_neg)))

if (is_in_column_name) {
  print("Alicyclobacillus is present in the column names.")
} else {
  print("Alicyclobacillus is not present in the column names.")
}
     
[1] "Alicyclobacillus is present in the column names."
Step 2: Align meta and taxa

# Ensure the row names of the meta match taxa
rownames(meta_fecal_neg) <- meta_fecal_neg$sampleID

# Match the rows of taxa with the order of SampleID in meta
taxa_fecal_neg <- taxa_fecal_neg[match(rownames(meta_fecal_neg), taxa_fecal_neg$sampleID), ]

# Check if row names now match
all.equal(rownames(meta_fecal_neg), rownames(taxa_fecal_neg))  # This should return TRUE
     
TRUE
Step 3: Convert to phyloseq object

# Remove 'sampleID' from taxa_fecal_neg as it's not part of the numeric matrix
taxa_fecal_neg$sampleID <- NULL

# Convert taxa_skin_neg to a matrix
RA_mat <- as.matrix(taxa_fecal_neg)

# Create the OTU table
phylo_RA <- otu_table(RA_mat, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)

# Create the sample data
phylo_samples <- sample_data(meta_fecal_neg)

# Create the phyloseq object
phylo_object <- phyloseq(phylo_RA, phylo_samples)
     
Step 4: Test components of the phyloseq object

#sample_sums(phylo_object)          # Total counts per sample
#sample_names(phylo_object)        # List of sample names
#sample_variables(phylo_object)    # Metadata variables
#otu_table(phylo_object)[1:3, 1:2] # First few OTU counts
     
Step 5: Indicate controls and identify contaminants

# Add a column to indicate negative controls
sample_data(phylo_object)$is.neg <- sample_data(phylo_object)$sample_or_control == "control"

# Identify contaminants using the prevalence method
contamdf.prev <- isContaminant(phylo_object, method = "prevalence", neg = "is.neg")

# Inspect results
table(contamdf.prev$contaminant)  # Count of contaminants
head(which(contamdf.prev$contaminant))  # Indices of contaminants
     
Step 6: Prevalence with spike-in

# Filter contaminants

contaminants_0.1 <- filter(contamdf.prev, contaminant == TRUE)
contaminants_0.1 <- tibble::rownames_to_column(contaminants_0.1, var = "species") 

# Save to CSV
write.csv(contaminants_0.1, "20250522_decon_fecal_prev_arranged_0.1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Number of identified contaminants (0.1) = 3
nrow(contaminants_0.1)

# Check if spike-in (Alicyclobacillus) is identified as contaminant
is_in_row_name <- any(grepl("Alicyclobacillus", rownames(contaminants_0.1)))

if (is_in_row_name) {
  print("Alicyclobacillus is present")
} else {
  print("Alicyclobacillus is not present")
}
     
Step 7: Frequency with spike-in

# Identifying contaminants using frequency method with constant DNA concentration
contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1 <- isContaminant(phylo_object, method="frequency", conc="fixed_dna_conc", threshold=0.1)  # threshold = 0.1
table(contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1$contaminant)
head(which(contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1$contaminant))

# Filter contaminants
contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1 <- filter(contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1, contaminant == TRUE)
contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1 <- tibble::rownames_to_column(contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1, var = "species")  # Add species column from rownames

# Save to CSV
write.csv(contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1, "20250522_decon_fecal_frequency_spike_0.1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Number of identified contaminants (0.1) = 0
nrow(contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1)

# Check if spike-in (Alicyclobacillus) is identified as contaminant
is_in_row_name <- any(grepl("Alicyclobacillus", rownames(contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1)))

if (is_in_row_name) {
  print("Alicyclobacillus is present")
} else {
  print("Alicyclobacillus is not present")
}
     
Step 8: Diff rows frequency and prevalence

#0.1

# Count diff = 3

# Count rows in contaminants_0.1 but not in contaminants_no_spike_0.1

# Count rows in contaminants_freq_0.1 but not in contaminants_0.1
n_diff_in_0.1 <- nrow(anti_join(contaminants_0.1, contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1, by = "species"))

# Print the result = 3
cat("Number of rows in contaminants_0.1 but not in contaminants_no_spike_0.1:", n_diff_in_0.1, "\n")

# Find rows in contaminants_0.1 but not in contaminants_freq_0.1
diff_species <- anti_join(contaminants_0.1, contamdf.freq_50ng_0.1, by = "species")

# Create diff dataframe
diff_species_spike_0.1 <- data.frame(diff_species)

# Save file
write.csv(diff_species_spike_0.1, "20250522_diff_decon_fecal_species_spike_0.1.csv")
     
Number of rows in contaminants_0.1 but not in contaminants_no_spike_0.1: 3 
Order false samples based on prev in negative controls

# Transform phyloseq object to presence-absence
ps.pa <- transform_sample_counts(phylo_object, function(abund) 1 * (abund > 0))

# Separate positive (true samples) and negative (controls)
ps.pa.neg <- prune_samples(sample_data(ps.pa)$sample_or_control == "control", ps.pa)
ps.pa.pos <- prune_samples(sample_data(ps.pa)$sample_or_control == "sample", ps.pa)

# Create a data frame of prevalence in positive and negative samples
df.pa <- data.frame(
  pa.pos = taxa_sums(ps.pa.pos),
  pa.neg = taxa_sums(ps.pa.neg),
  contaminant = contamdf.prev$contaminant
)
     

# Step 1: Filter contaminants with species column
contaminants_0.1 <- contamdf.prev %>%
  filter(contaminant == TRUE) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "species")  # Add species column from row names

# Step 2: Create a data frame of prevalence in positive and negative samples
df.pa <- data.frame(
  species = rownames(contamdf.prev),  # Add species column
  pa.pos = taxa_sums(ps.pa.pos),
  pa.neg = taxa_sums(ps.pa.neg),
  contaminant = contamdf.prev$contaminant
)

# Step 3: Merge the prevalence data with the filtered contaminants
true_contaminants_sorted <- contaminants_0.1 %>%
  inner_join(df.pa, by = c("species", "contaminant")) %>%  # Match on species and contaminant
  arrange(pa.neg)  # Order by prevalence in negative controls (pa.neg)

# Step 4: Display and save the result
print(true_contaminants_sorted)
write.csv(true_contaminants_sorted, "20250522_prev_pa_neg.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Step 4: Display and save the table
print(true_contaminants_sorted)

# Filter and sort the data for true contaminants
true_samples_sorted <- df.pa %>%
  filter(contaminant == TRUE) %>%
  arrange(pa.neg)  # Order by prevalence in negative controls (pa.neg)

# Display the table
print(true_samples_sorted)

# Save the table to a CSV file
write.csv(true_samples_sorted, "true_samples_sorted_by_pa_neg.csv", row.names = FALSE)
     
# Extract neg and skin samples from taxa

# Filter taxa_neg to keep only rows where sampleID matches meta_fecal_neg
taxa_fecal_neg <- taxa_neg %>%
  filter(sampleID %in% meta_fecal_neg$sampleID)
     
Decontam without spike
Step 1: Create dataframe without spike

taxa_fecal_neg_no_spike <- taxa_fecal_neg[, !grepl("Alicyclobacillus", colnames(taxa_fecal_neg))]
     
Step 2: Align meta and taxa

# Ensure the row names of the meta match taxa
rownames(meta_fecal_neg) <- meta_fecal_neg$sampleID

# Match the rows of taxa with the order of SampleID in meta
taxa_fecal_neg_no_spike <- taxa_fecal_neg_no_spike[match(rownames(meta_fecal_neg), taxa_fecal_neg_no_spike$sampleID), ]

# Check if row names now match
all.equal(rownames(meta_fecal_neg), rownames(taxa_fecal_neg_no_spike))  # This should return TRUE
     
TRUE
Step 3: Convert to phyloseq object

# Remove 'sampleID' from taxa_skin_neg as it's not part of the numeric matrix
taxa_fecal_neg_no_spike$sampleID <- NULL

# Convert taxa_skin_neg to a matrix
RA_mat <- as.matrix(taxa_fecal_neg_no_spike)

# Create the OTU table
phylo_RA <- otu_table(RA_mat, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)

# Create the sample data
phylo_samples <- sample_data(meta_fecal_neg)

# Create the phyloseq object
phylo_object <- phyloseq(phylo_RA, phylo_samples)
     
Step 4: Test components of phyloseq object

#sample_sums(phylo_object)          # Total counts per sample
#sample_names(phylo_object)        # List of sample names
#sample_variables(phylo_object)    # Metadata variables
#otu_table(phylo_object)[1:3, 1:2] # First few OTU counts
     
Step 5: Indicate controls and identify contaminants

# Add a column to indicate negative controls
sample_data(phylo_object)$is.neg <- sample_data(phylo_object)$sample_or_control == "control"

# Identify contaminants using the prevalence method
contamdf.prev <- isContaminant(phylo_object, method = "prevalence", neg = "is.neg")

# Inspect results
table(contamdf.prev$contaminant)  # Count of contaminants
head(which(contamdf.prev$contaminant))  # Indices of contaminants
     
Step 6: Prevalence without spike-in

# Filter contaminants
contaminants_no_spike_0.1 <- contamdf.prev %>%
  filter(contaminant == TRUE) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "species")  # Add species column from rownames

# Save to CSV
write.csv(contaminants_no_spike_0.1, "20250522_decon_fecal_prevalence_no_spike_0.1.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Number of identified contaminants (0.1) = 3
nrow(contaminants_no_spike_0.1)

# Count diff

# Count rows in contaminants_0.1 but not in contaminants_no_spike_0.1
n_diff_in_0.1 <- nrow(anti_join(contaminants_0.1, contaminants_no_spike_0.1, by = "species"))

# Print the result
cat("Number of rows in contaminants_0.1 but not in contaminants_no_spike_0.1:", n_diff_in_0.1, "\n")
     
Number of rows in contaminants_0.1 but not in contaminants_no_spike_0.1: 0 
Step 7: Frequency without spike-in

# Set the thresholds you want to test
thresholds <- c(0.2, 0.3, 0.4, 0.5)

# Initialize a list to store results
results <- list()

# Loop through each threshold
for (t in thresholds) {
  # Run isContaminant with the current threshold
  contam_result <- isContaminant(
    phylo_object,
    method = "frequency",
    conc = "fixed_dna_conc",
    threshold = t
  )
  
  # Check if the "contaminant" column is present and contains TRUE/FALSE
  if (!"contaminant" %in% colnames(contam_result)) {
    stop("The 'contaminant' column is missing from isContaminant results.")
  }
  
  # Filter contaminants (TRUE values) and add species names
  filtered_contaminants <- contam_result %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "species") %>%
    filter(contaminant == TRUE)
  
  # Save the filtered contaminants for this threshold
  results[[paste0("threshold_", t)]] <- filtered_contaminants
  
  # Print number of contaminants for debugging
  cat("Threshold:", t, "Number of contaminants:", nrow(filtered_contaminants), "\n")
  
  # Save to CSV
  write.csv(
    filtered_contaminants,
    file = paste0("decon_freq_skin_", t, ".csv"),
    row.names = FALSE
  )
}


# Combine all results into one summary table for comparison
summary_table <- bind_rows(
  lapply(names(results), function(name) {
    data.frame(
      Threshold = sub("threshold_", "", name),
      Num_Contaminants = nrow(results[[name]])
    )
  })
)

# Save the summary table
write.csv(summary_table, "decon_threshold_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
print(summary_table)
